title: [083-agentmind-extraction-v2] Bead 1: Phase 0 prerequisite gate — verify agentmind v0.0.1 and record SHA
description:
Gates every later bead. No mindspec code changes; this is a verification +
spec amendment. **Scope note:** v0.0.1 is a scaffold-only tag per spec Phase
0 (lines 312-313); this bead does NOT gate live-capture on a runnable binary
from v0.0.1. Live-capture verification (Test D Phase-4+) is gated on the
later v0.3.0 tag in Bead 4.

**Steps**
1. Run `git ls-remote --tags https://github.com/mrmaxsteel/agentmind | grep 'refs/tags/v0.0.1$'`
   (spec Test G). Capture the SHA.
2. Clone the agentmind repo at the v0.0.1 tag to a scratch directory and run
   `go list -m -json all | jq -r '.Path' | grep '^github.com/mrmaxsteel/mindspec'`
   to confirm no mindspec dep (spec Test B). Expect zero matches.
3. Probe the v0.0.1 tag for binary scaffolding. If `cmd/agentmind/main.go`
   exists at the tag, build it and run `./bin/agentmind --version | grep
   '^agentmind'` (spec Test A). If `cmd/agentmind/` is absent at v0.0.1
   (scaffold-only Phase 0), record this finding in the commit message and
   defer spec Test A to the first tag that publishes a binary (v0.2.0 or
   v0.3.0 per spec Phase 3/4); the bead remains green on the scaffold-only
   outcome.
4. Edit `spec.md`: replace the `agentmind v0.0.1 SHA: <TBD — record before Phase 1>`
   placeholder with the actual SHA captured in step 1. Single commit.

**Verification**
- [ ] `git ls-remote` output for the v0.0.1 tag is captured in the commit message.
- [ ] Test B and Test G pass against the v0.0.1 tag.
- [ ] Test A either passes (if v0.0.1 produces a binary) or is documented
      as deferred to the binary-publishing tag (if v0.0.1 is scaffold-only).
- [ ] `spec.md` no longer contains the `<TBD>` placeholder.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] Spec Test G (agentmind v0.0.1 tag exists) passes and the SHA is
      recorded in `spec.md`.
- [ ] Spec Test B (no mindspec dep in agentmind) passes against the v0.0.1
      tag.
- [ ] Spec Test A (standalone-binary exists) either passes against v0.0.1
      or is explicitly deferred (with the deferral target tag recorded) per
      the scaffold-only Phase 0 contract.
- [ ] Spec AC "agentmind v0.0.1 SHA recorded before Phase 1" is satisfied;
      no `<TBD>` placeholder remains.

**Depends on**
None (but blocks Beads 2–7).

| `internal/viz/graph.go` | `internal/viz/graph.go` | 475 | Graph state machine. |
| `internal/viz/normalize.go` | `internal/viz/normalize.go` | — | Imports `wire.CollectedEvent` instead of `bench.CollectedEvent`. |
| `internal/viz/live.go` | `internal/viz/live.go` | 306 | OTLP → graph live pipeline. |
| `internal/viz/replay.go` | `internal/viz/replay.go` | 200 | NDJSON replay. |
| `internal/viz/codex_fallback.go` | `internal/viz/codex_fallback.go` | 464 | Codex JSONL converter. |
| `internal/viz/run.go` | `internal/viz/run.go` | — | `RunLiveOpts` entrypoint. |
| `internal/viz/web/*` (embed.FS assets) | `internal/viz/web/*` | — | UI bundle. |
| (new) | `cmd/agentmind/main.go` | ~150 | Standalone binary with `serve`, `replay`, `setup` subcommands. Mirrors current `mindspec agentmind` cobra tree. |
| (new) | `cmd/agentmind/serve.go` | ~60 | `serve` subcommand handler — calls `viz.RunLiveOpts`. |
| (new) | `cmd/agentmind/replay.go` | ~80 | `replay` subcommand handler. |
| (new) | `client/client.go` | ~60 | Thin client API: `AutoStart`, `IsRunning`, `DefaultOTLPPort`, `DefaultUIPort`, `ReadEvents(io.Reader) <-chan wire.CollectedEvent`, and the typed sentinel `ErrBinaryNotFound`. Imports only stdlib and `agentmind/wire`. |

**Stays in `mindspec/`:**

| Path | Notes |
|------|-------|
| `internal/bench/runner.go` | Orchestrates benches. Reads NDJSON output written by agentmind subprocess; no longer parses OTLP itself. |
| `internal/bench/session.go` | Reads NDJSON via `wire.CollectedEvent` (imported from agentmind). |
| `internal/recording/collector.go` | Replaces direct OTLP parsing with: spawn agentmind via `client.AutoStart`, read its NDJSON output. |
| `cmd/mindspec/viz.go` | Thin shell. `mindspec agentmind serve` re-execs `agentmind serve` with the same flags. If agentmind binary is missing, prints the graceful-degradation warning and exits 0. |
| `cmd/mindspec/record.go`, `cmd/mindspec/bench.go` | Unchanged for end users. Internally call the new boundary. |
| `internal/agentmind/*` | **Deleted in Phase 5.** Spec failure if the directory still exists from the Phase 5 deletion commit onward. |

**Becomes shared between both repos:**

| Module | Owner | Consumers |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| `github.com/mrmaxsteel/agentmind/wire` | agentmind | mindspec (read NDJSON, deserialize events) + agentmind internal |

## ADR Decisions

### ADR-0011

**AgentMind is the single OTLP receiver for all telemetry.** There is no separate headless collector. Port 4318 is the only OTLP port. AgentMind handles:

1. **Live visualization** (existing capability)
2. **NDJSON recording to disk** (new: `--output` flag)
3. **Benchmark collection** (new: auto-started by `bench run`)
4. **Per-spec recording** (new: auto-started by `record start` / `spec-init`)

When AgentMind is not running and telemetry arrives, the OTLP exporter silently drops events — this is standard OpenTelemetry behavior (connection refused, no retry storm, no visible errors). Nothing breaks.

### Key Changes from ADR-0010

| ADR-0010 Decision | ADR-0011 Replacement |
|:-------------------|:---------------------|
| Separate recording collector on :4319 | AgentMind on :4318 handles all collection |
| `record collect` as detached bg process | `agentmind serve --output <path>` as detached bg process |
| OTLP endpoint configured to :4319 | OTLP endpoint stays at :4318 |
| Bench collector on :4320 | Eliminated — bench uses AgentMind |
| No live viz during recording/bench | Live viz always available when AgentMind is running |

### What Stays the Same from ADR-0010

- **Spec-scoped recordings** at `docs/specs/<id>/recording/` — unchanged
- **Single append-only NDJSON file** per spec — unchanged
- **Phase markers** (`lifecycle.start`, `lifecycle.phase`, etc.) — unchanged
- **Manifest schema** — unchanged (PID now points to AgentMind process)
- **Zero-friction automatic capture** — unchanged (auto-start moves from `record collect` to `agentmind serve`)
- **SessionStart hook health-check** — unchanged (checks AgentMind PID instead)

### Auto-Start Behavior

Commands that need telemetry collection auto-start AgentMind if it is not already running:

| Command | Action |
|:--------|:-------|
| `spec-init` | Start AgentMind with `--output <spec-recording-dir>/events.ndjson` |
| `bench run` | Start AgentMind with `--output <bench-dir>/bench-events.jsonl` |
| `record start` | Start AgentMind with `--output <spec-recording-dir>/events.ndjson` |
| SessionStart hook | Health-check, restart if dead |

All print: `AgentMind started — watch live at http://localhost:8420`

This means operators always have a visualization URL available. They don't have to know about it or use it — but when they want to see what's happening, the UI is one click away.

### ADR-0026

- **Repo location:** AgentMind lives at `github.com/mrmaxsteel/agentmind`.
- **Import surface from mindspec:** `mindspec` imports only
  `github.com/mrmaxsteel/agentmind/client` (process lifecycle: `AutoStart`,
  `RunStandalone`, `ReadEvents`, typed sentinel `ErrBinaryNotFound`) and
  `github.com/mrmaxsteel/agentmind/wire` (canonical NDJSON
  encoder/decoder, normalization tool, types).
- **IPC channels:** OTLP/HTTP:4318 is the inbound channel (mindspec writers
  → agentmind ingestor); NDJSON-over-stdout is the outbound channel
  (agentmind subprocess → mindspec readers via `client.ReadEvents` on the
  subprocess stdout pipe — never via file-tailing the `--output` path).
- **Per-class degradation contract** when the agentmind binary is absent:
  - *telemetry-as-output* (`mindspec record start`): non-zero exit.
  - *batch* (`mindspec bench run`, `mindspec agentmind setup`): exit 0 with
    exactly one centralized `sync.Once` warn line emitted from
    `agentmind/client`.
  - *interactive* (`mindspec viz`, `agentmind serve`, `agentmind replay`):
    non-zero exit.
- **Binary-not-found detection:** `errors.Is(err, client.ErrBinaryNotFound)`
  at every call site. Substring-matching on error message text is
  prohibited and enforced by a unit-test assertion at each rewired call
  site.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Spec Test G (agentmind v0.0.1 tag exists) passes and the SHA is
recorded in `spec.md`.
- [ ] Spec Test B (no mindspec dep in agentmind) passes against the v0.0.1
tag.
- [ ] Spec Test A (standalone-binary exists) either passes against v0.0.1
or is explicitly deferred (with the deferral target tag recorded) per
the scaffold-only Phase 0 contract.
- [ ] Spec AC "agentmind v0.0.1 SHA recorded before Phase 1" is satisfied;
no `<TBD>` placeholder remains.
close_reason: Closed

title: [SPEC 083-agentmind-extraction-v2] Agentmind Extraction V2
close_reason: all steps complete

title: [SEC-4] agentmind autostart: verify it's actually AgentMind before trusting
description:
`internal/agentmind/autostart.go:23-38` `IsRunning(port)` just probes the TCP port. Any other local process listening on 4318 (malicious local user, poorly-secured Docker container exposing 4318) is silently trusted; subsequent telemetry from Claude Code / Codex flows to it instead of AgentMind. If `log_user_prompt` is enabled anywhere in the chain, that telemetry includes prompts.
Note: viz/AgentMind is being extracted into its own component, but the autostart trigger and discovery logic remain on the mindspec side. The trust check still applies regardless of where the receiver lives.
design: Probe with an OTLP no-op POST and verify the response contains an AgentMind-specific marker, or write a one-time token + PID to `~/.mindspec/agentmind.lock` and require the listener to expose the token via a known sidechannel before reuse.
close_reason: SEC-4 moot: internal/agentmind/ deleted entirely (d71c3ab, ADR-0026 extraction) — no autostart/port-probe/trust code remains mindspec-side. Verified during spec 097 draft.

title: [ZFC-7] Stop regex-extracting ADR IDs from spec prose; use PlanFrontmatter.ADRCitations
description:
`internal/approve/plan.go:464-479` `parseADRIDs` runs `ADR-(\d{4})` against the spec's `## ADR Touchpoints` markdown to assemble the per-bead "Design" field. `PlanFrontmatter.ADRCitations` already exists (`validate/plan.go:25-26`) — two sources of truth. A spec that writes "see ADR-9999" yields a phantom citation; one that writes "see the security ADR (decisions/adr-0023.md)" yields nothing.
Consume only `plan.adr_citations` from frontmatter. Have the template tell the agent to populate it.
notes: Re-review 2026-05-14: reclassified from ZFC to architecture. parseADRIDs is mechanical extraction of a known token format from prose — parsing, not judgment. ZFC doesn't forbid that. The actual issue is two sources of truth: PlanFrontmatter.ADRCitations already exists in structured form, so this is duplicative parsing. Pick one source of truth.
close_reason: ZFC-7 fixed by spec 097 R2 (PR #142): bead --design ADR list now built from structured PlanFrontmatter.ADRCitations; prose adrIDRe/parseADRIDs retired.

title: [PERF-3] Defer hook.ReadState until after branch-protection short-circuit
description:
`internal/hook/hook.go:200-238` `ReadState()` calls `phase.ResolveContext(root)` → bd fan-out. `cmd/mindspec/hook.go:72` calls `hook.ReadState()` before `Run()`. For pre-commit hooks on non-protected branches, `runPreCommit` returns Pass immediately — but `ReadState()` has already paid the bd cost.
Restructure so branch-protection runs first; defer state resolution until needed. Combined with PERF-1, pre-commit hooks become near-free on non-protected branches.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #119 (lazy stateFn provider in hook.Run; state resolved only for protected/spec branches). Panel: 3/3 APPROVE (correctness path-enumeration, adversarial bypass, mutation-tested pins), 2026-06-10

title: [PERF-4] Drop bd ListJSON human-output fallback or fail loudly
description: `internal/bead/bdcli.go:182-226` falls back to parsing `bd list` human output line-by-line when `--json` "fails", and then fires `bd show <id> --json` for every ID it finds. A single "list 30 ready beads" balloons into 30 subprocesses, silently — indistinguishable from a hang.
design: Either (a) require a minimum `bd` version that supports `--json` (the doctor already version-checks at `internal/doctor/beads.go:317`) and remove the fallback, or (b) detect the fallback firing once at startup, log loudly via `ui.RenderWarn`, and refuse to retry.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #90 commit 786287f; ListJSON returns structured error, design option (a); verified 2026-06-10

title: [ARCH-7] Consolidate worktree path helpers into internal/workspace
description:
The literal `.worktrees`, `worktree-spec-`, and bead-worktree path patterns appear in at least 6 packages even though `config.WorktreeRoot` claims to be the source of truth:
- `internal/state/state.go:82,88` (`SpecWorktreePath`, `BeadWorktreePath`)
- `internal/workspace/workspace.go:141`
- `internal/executor/mindspec_executor.go:232,278`
- `internal/complete/complete.go:144`
- `internal/harness/sandbox.go:271`
design: Move `state.SpecBranch`, `state.SpecWorktreePath`, `state.BeadWorktreePath` to `internal/workspace`. Have them read `config.WorktreeRoot` exactly once. Replace literal-string call sites with workspace helpers.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #107 (internal/workspace/worktree.go); harness keeps .worktrees literal by design; verified 2026-06-10

title: [ZFC-4] Drop bead step-count <3 AddError (lower-bound is opinionated gating)
description:
`internal/validate/plan.go:383-411` `checkBeadSection` enforces "3-7 steps per bead" — an error below 3, a warning above 7. This is an opinion about good plan granularity, not a structural invariant. A two-step bead ("write the migration; run it") may be appropriate; a tightly-coupled nine-step refactor bead may be appropriate. Encoding the project's preferred sizing as a hard validation error blocks plan approval on a heuristic.
Drop both the lower-bound error and upper-bound warning. Keep "must have at least one step" if a structural check is needed. Bead sizing is a plan-review judgment.
notes: Re-review 2026-05-14: narrowed scope. The upper-bound (> 7 → AddWarning) is taste but advisory, so it's defensible. The lower-bound (< 3 → AddError) is the actual ZFC concern: it BLOCKS plan approval because of an opinion about granularity. A two-step bead ('write migration; run it') may be appropriate. Action: change the AddError to AddWarning (or drop entirely). Keep 'must have at least one step' as the only structural gate.
close_reason: ZFC-4 done: internal/validate/plan.go demoted step-count<3 from AddError to AddWarning (600f620); only StepsCount==0 remains a structural-floor error. Verified during spec 097 m557 re-audit.

title: [ZFC-3] Drop isPlaceholder keyword sniff from validate/spec.go
description:
`internal/validate/spec.go:100-119` `isPlaceholder` checks `strings.Contains(text, "<Brief description")` etc. against a hand-curated list of English template stubs. The instant any template phrasing is edited, agents paraphrase the stub, or the project is used in a non-English deployment, this silently passes garbage. It is a semantic judgment ("did the agent actually fill this in?") that belongs to the AI reviewer.
Either drop it entirely (rely on the structural "section exists with non-whitespace content" check that's already there), or use a sentinel comment (`<!-- mindspec-placeholder -->`) that the agent must remove — structural and explicit rather than English-keyword scanning.
close_reason: Re-review: not actually a ZFC violation. isPlaceholder checks for literal strings like '<Brief description' that mindspec itself emits as scaffolding stubs. It's a structural fingerprint check against mindspec's own artifact format, not semantic content analysis. ZFC explicitly allows structural safety checks.

title: [ZFC-1] Review default thresholds in checkDecompositionQuality (warnings, not gates)
description:
`internal/validate/plan.go:437-542` `checkDecompositionQuality` encodes hand-rolled numeric thresholds (bead count > 6, `R_scope > 0.50`, chain depth > 3, parallelism < 0.25) with embedded research-paper citations. "Is this decomposition any good?" is a semantic judgment about chunking quality, not a structural invariant. The thresholds also rely on `ExtractPathRefs`/`beadDepRe` regex-scanning English prose for file paths and dependencies — so a bead phrased differently produces wildly different scores. A few lines above (lines 175-180, 394-400), the same file documents removing verification-keyword sniffs as ZFC violations, then this scorer was added next to them.
Drop the function. Move the rubric (if wanted) into the LLM review step at plan-approve time, or keep it only in the `instruct/templates/plan.md` template for the model to reason about.
notes: Re-review 2026-05-14: scope narrowed. The function emits AddWarning only — it's advisory, not a gate; LLM/human reviewer still decides. The underlying metrics (bead count, R_scope, chain depth, parallelism) are purely structural. The remaining concern is whether the threshold values (0.50, 0.15, 0.25, 3, 6) are still backed by current data and whether warnings at these defaults add signal or noise. Action: periodic review of thresholds, or move them to config so they can be tuned per-project. Do NOT delete the function — surfacing structural metrics to the reviewer is ZFC-compliant.
close_reason: ZFC-1 moot: checkDecompositionQuality emits AddWarning only (never gates) and all 5 thresholds are config-driven via config.Decomposition with default-backfill — exactly the re-review's ask; do-not-delete honored. Verified during spec 097 re-audit.

title: [SEC-2] O_NOFOLLOW / pre-Lstat protection on writes to user-config files
description: `internal/setup/claude.go:431`, `internal/setup/copilot.go:83`, and `internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go:163` open user-config files with `os.OpenFile(absPath, os.O_APPEND|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)` and follow symlinks. If an attacker can plant a symlink at `./CLAUDE.md` (e.g., in a repo the user clones, or in a shared dev container) pointing at e.g. `~/.ssh/authorized_keys`, `mindspec setup claude` appends the managed block to the target.
design: Before opening, `os.Lstat` the path; if it's a symlink, refuse with a clear error telling the user to remove it. On platforms where the syscall is available, use `O_NOFOLLOW`. For full-file writes, write to a tempfile in the same directory and atomically `rename` (the pattern already used in `internal/bead/config.go:395`).
close_reason: SEC-2 already remediated: internal/safeio/safeopen{,_unix}.go (Lstat pre-check + O_NOFOLLOW + tempfile-rename); all cited write sites call safeio.WriteFileNoSymlink/OpenAppendNoSymlink (fix d338c1a). Verified open→fixed during spec 097 draft.

title: [ARCH-12] Move state.CrossValidate and warning types to internal/validate
description:
`internal/state/` is a grab-bag: mode constants + Focus DTO + session JSON I/O + worktree path helpers + `CrossValidate` drift warnings. Five responsibilities in one package.
Path helpers are addressed in ARCH-7. This bead handles the cross-validation move: relocate `state.CrossValidate` and `state.Warning` to `internal/validate/state.go`. After this and ARCH-7, `state` shrinks to its actual responsibility (session/focus persistence + mode constants).
close_reason: Shipped via PR #91 commits eb8f0ac/ddb3334; CrossValidate at internal/validate/state.go:24; verified 2026-06-10

title: [ARCH-6] Extract canonical internal/frontmatter package; remove the 3 ad-hoc parsers
description:
`internal/validate/frontmatter.go` is explicitly the canonical YAML helper (comment at line 17 documents the ZFC rationale). Yet three other packages reimplement it:
- `internal/state/validate.go:195-214` — `bufio.Scanner` parser returning `"unknown"`
- `internal/speclist/speclist.go:66-89` — near-identical copy
- `internal/instruct/instruct.go:225-249` — `bytes.Split` parser comparing against literal `"Approved"` (fails on lowercase, unquoted, etc.)
design:
1. Lift `validate.frontmatter.go` out into a new top-level `internal/frontmatter` package (so `state` can import it without a layering inversion).
2. Expose `Status(bytes)` and `Field(bytes, key)`.
3. Replace the four ad-hoc parsers with calls into the new package.
4. Re-export through `validate` for backward compatibility if anything outside the module depends on the current location.
close_reason: Shipped via commit 5315acf; internal/frontmatter package exists, 3 ad-hoc parsers removed; verified 2026-06-10

title: [ZFC-6] Move bead-dependency declarations from prose regex to structured frontmatter
description:
`internal/approve/plan.go:340-366` and `internal/validate/plan.go:497-521` both use `(?i)bead\s+(\d+)` to scrape dependencies out of a "Depends on" markdown paragraph. The contract is implicit: write "Bead N" literally or your dep is silently dropped. Anything paraphrased ("the formatter bead", "Bead 3 and Bead 5 but only after migration") is missed without error.
Add a structured frontmatter field (`depends_on: [1, 3]`) per bead, parse only that, and update plan.md template + validation accordingly.
notes: Re-review 2026-05-14: reclassified from ZFC to architecture. Parsing 'Bead 2, Bead 5' from prose to wire dep edges is mechanical parsing of a microformat, not a semantic judgment. ZFC allows parsing. The real issue is API contract: the format is implicit, so paraphrasing silently breaks dep wiring. The fix (move to structured frontmatter) is still right but the framing was wrong.
close_reason: ZFC-6 fixed by spec 097 R3 (PR #142): bead dependencies now declared via structured work_chunks frontmatter; both prose bead-dep regexes removed.

title: [ZFC-5] Replace path-from-prose extractors with structured plan frontmatter
description:
`internal/contextpack/builder.go:36-78` `ExtractFilePathsFromText` and `internal/harness/analyzer.go:679-721` `extractMentionedPaths` recover "which files a bead touches" by regex-scanning English prose, hard-coding Go-specific prefixes (`internal/`, `cmd/`, `pkg/`) and extensions. A Rust/TypeScript project yields zero paths. The output is then surfaced authoritatively in the bead context primer as "Key File Paths" (`contextpack/beadctx.go:79-91`).
Move "key file paths" into a structured plan-frontmatter field (or per-bead structured block) that the agent writes explicitly. Drop the regex extractors.
notes: Re-review 2026-05-14: reclassified from ZFC to architecture. ExtractFilePathsFromText is opportunistic enrichment for context packs, not a gate. The output is 'here are files possibly relevant — agent, look if you want.' That's not a judgment call in code; it's parsing. The real concern is the hardcoded Go-specific prefix list (internal/, cmd/, pkg/) being non-portable. Worst case is an incomplete context pack, not a wrong decision. Reframed: 'Make path enrichment toolchain-agnostic' rather than 'remove the regex.'
close_reason: ZFC-5 primary site fixed by spec 097 R4 (PR #142): contextpack ## Key File Paths now sourced from structured key_file_paths frontmatter; dead ExtractFilePathsFromText deleted. Harness-site residual tracked in mindspec-qcjf.

title: [ARCH-10] Split internal/harness/scenario.go (2525 LOC) into focused files
description: `internal/harness/scenario.go` has grown to 2,525 LOC containing 26 scenario builders, 25 assertion helpers, 5 stray placeholder functions (`Greet`, `Divide`, `Done`, `First`, `Process`, two inline `main`s), and a TODO at line 356 (`// TODO: finish this function`).
design:
Split into:
- `internal/harness/scenarios/spec_lifecycle.go` (SpecInit/Approve/PlanApprove/ImplApprove)
- `internal/harness/scenarios/bead_lifecycle.go` (SingleBead/MultiBeadDeps/Complete)
- `internal/harness/scenarios/safety.go` (Bugfix, BlockedBead, UnmergedBeadGuard, StopAfterComplete)
- `internal/harness/scenarios/worktree.go` (StaleWorktree, ApproveFromWorktree, MultipleActiveSpecs)
- `internal/harness/asserts.go` (all `assertX` helpers + `mustRunGit`)

Drop the 5 stray placeholder funcs. Address the TODO or delete the function.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #104; scenario.go now 52 LOC split into 5 files; the 'stray funcs' were sandbox fixture string literals, false positive in original review; verified 2026-06-10

title: [ARCH-9] Add missing gitutil helpers and replace ~30 raw exec.Command("git",...) sites
description:
`internal/gitutil` already centralizes git invocations through a swappable `execCommand`, but ~30 sites bypass it with raw `exec.Command("git", ...)`:
- `internal/bench/{session,runner,worktree,qualitative}.go` (10+ sites)
- `internal/doctor/{git,beads}.go` (5+ sites: `git ls-files`, `git rm`, `git check-ignore`)
- `internal/next/guard.go:30`
- `internal/validate/docsync.go:48`
- `internal/hook/dispatch.go:107` (`getCurrentBranch` duplicates `gitutil.CurrentBranch`)
Net effect: branch-resolution logic is implemented twice; tests of `bench`/`doctor`/`next`/`hook` can't run hermetically without a real `git`. This also unlocks SEC-5 (the `--` separator fix lands in one place).
design:
1. Add `gitutil.Status(workdir) ([]byte, error)`, `gitutil.LsFiles(args...) ([]byte, error)`, `gitutil.RmCached(...)`, `gitutil.CheckIgnore(...)`, `gitutil.DiffNameOnly(workdir, ref) ([]string, error)`, `gitutil.RevParseHEAD(...)`.
2. Switch all bypass sites. Leave `internal/harness` alone (test harness; direct shelling is acceptable there).
3. Add hermetic tests using the existing `execCommand` swap.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #105; harness exempt by design, 3 executor sites out of bead scope; verified 2026-06-10

title: Code review 2026-05-14: ZFC, security, performance, architecture findings
description:
Umbrella epic for findings from the multi-agent codebase review on 2026-05-14. Four parallel review agents audited mindspec (excluding vendored beads/): ZFC (Zero Framework Cognition), security, performance, and architecture/refactoring.

Pure-viz findings were intentionally excluded since viz is being extracted into its own component. Borderline viz items (e.g. NDJSON write patterns, agentmind autostart) are kept only where the mindspec-side concern persists.

All children carry the label `code-review-2026-05-14` for filtering. See child issues for individual findings with file:line citations.
close_reason: All children resolved: ~15 findings were already-fixed (closed during the 2026-06-13 re-audit) and the 7 genuinely-open survivors shipped in spec 097 (PR #142). Residuals tracked in mindspec-qcjf (harness paths) + mindspec-0wxh (tree-wide git-exec audit).

title: bd 1.0.2 sidecar dolt server in mindspec worktrees (redirect gap)
description:
From spec 082 bead 4 (mindspec-hzdh.10) spike diagnosis: when mindspec creates a worktree via bd worktree create, bd 1.0.2 does NOT fall back to git common-dir discovery as its help text claims. Because .beads/metadata.json and .beads/config.yaml are tracked in git, a fresh worktree checkout contains them — bd sees a local .beads/ and spawns its own sidecar Dolt server from the worktree's .beads/, which immediately fails (empty dolt/ directory). All bd commands from inside the worktree error with 'database not found on Dolt server at 127.0.0.1:<port>'. Affects mindspec spec create, mindspec next, and any agent workflow run inside a worktree.

Fix surfaces (pick one): (a) stop tracking .beads/metadata.json in git so worktrees are bare and trigger bd's common-dir fallback; (b) after bd worktree create, explicitly clear the worktree's .beads/ runtime files and verify fallback kicks in; (c) upstream fix in bd 1.0.3 to prefer common-dir over local .beads/ when the local one has no dolt/ directory. Option (a) is the cleanest — metadata.json is per-project identity that belongs on main only.

The regression test TestWorktreeCreate_SharesMainDB in internal/bead/bdcli_test.go is skipped pending this fix; removing the skip should be the first commit of this bead.

Depends on: mindspec-hzdh.10 (EnsureBeadsConfig wiring — does not itself fix this bug but surfaces it cleanly).
close_reason: Obsolete: bd 1.0.4 embedded mode spawns no sidecar Dolt server; TestWorktreeCreate_SharesMainDB passes un-skipped (verified 2026-06-10); skip removed in this commit

title: [082-beads-artifact-handling] Bead 5: Doctor checks for beads/mindspec integration health
description:
Applies ADR-0023/ADR-0025 (doctor surfaces deviations from the single-state-authority + projection model).

**Steps**
1. Extend `internal/doctor/beads.go` with four checks, each returning a `doctor.Check` result:
   - `checkBeadsConfigDrift(root)`: calls a new read-only variant of the required-keys scan from Bead 3 (share the required-keys constant, don't rewrite). Reports each missing or drifted key.
   - `checkStrayRootJSONL(root)`: runs `git ls-files --full-name -- issues.jsonl` at the repo root. If non-empty, warns and suggests `git rm --cached issues.jsonl` (cleanup across branches is out of scope; see spec).
   - `checkDurabilityRisk(root)`: reads `.beads/config.yaml` for `export.auto`. Detects "Dolt remote configured" by (in order, falling back): reading `sync.remote` from `.beads/config.yaml`, reading `.beads/dolt/.dolt/config.json` for any configured remote, or shelling out `bd` and scraping whatever remote-listing surface the installed version exposes. If `export.auto: false` AND no remote is detected, warns with remediation. If remote detection itself fails, skip the check with an info-level note rather than false-positive warn.
   - `checkBdVersionFloor(root)`: runs `bd --version` and parses with regex `\bv?([0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+)` (handles `bd version 1.0.2 (Homebrew)`-style output and `v1.0.2` alike). On parse failure, skip the check with "unknown bd version" info — do not false-warn. On successful parse < 1.0.2, warn.
2. Add a `--fix` mode (or extend existing) on `mindspec doctor` that, for `checkBeadsConfigDrift`, calls `EnsureBeadsConfig(root, force=false)` and surfaces the `ConfigResult`. `--fix --force` passes `force=true`. If mindspec doctor already has a different `--fix` surface, integrate; otherwise add the flag and wire it.
3. Unit tests for each check using temp dirs + fake `bd` on `PATH`. Include a test for Check 4's graceful-degradation-on-unknown-version-format path.
4. **Bead review finding**: add a test that asserts `--fix --force` replaces user-authored `export.git-add: true` while `--fix` alone preserves it (this complements Bead 3's unit coverage by asserting the doctor wiring honors the `force` flag).
5. Integration test: run `mindspec doctor` on a scratch repo with default `bd init` → reports config drift; run `mindspec doctor --fix` → patches; re-run → clean.

**Verification**
- [ ] `go test ./internal/doctor/...` passes new tests.
- [ ] Manual smoke: `mindspec doctor` on this repo (which already has the mindspec keys) reports clean beads section.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] Spec AC "`mindspec doctor` on a bd-default repo reports each missing mindspec key" — covered by integration test.
- [ ] Spec AC "`mindspec doctor --fix` patches; re-running reports clean" — covered by integration test.
- [ ] Spec AC "`mindspec doctor` on a repo with tracked root `issues.jsonl` warns" — covered by unit test.
- [ ] `checkDurabilityRisk` fires exactly when `export.auto: false` AND no Dolt remote is detected, and gracefully skips when remote detection itself fails.
- [ ] `checkBdVersionFloor` fires on parseable versions < 1.0.2, skips on parse failure, passes on >= 1.0.2.
- [ ] `--fix --force` replaces user-authored `export.git-add: true`; `--fix` alone preserves it.

design:
## Requirements

### R1: Treat `.beads/issues.jsonl` as a build artifact in the dirty-tree guard

The dirty-tree guard in `mindspec next` (currently in `internal/next/`) must distinguish build-artifact dirt from user-work dirt.

1. Snapshot the set of dirty paths (staged + unstaged) via `git status --porcelain`.
2. If `.beads/issues.jsonl` appears in the dirty set:
   - Run `bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl` to normalize the diff to current Dolt state (defensive against stale throttled exports).
   - Re-check `git status`. The JSONL may still be dirty (legitimate issue changes) or may have gone back to clean (throttled auto-export was writing stale content).
3. If the **only** remaining dirty path is `.beads/issues.jsonl`, proceed with the bead claim. The guard's job is to prevent losing user code/docs — it must not block on auto-generated files.
4. If other paths are also dirty, preserve current behavior (abort with "uncommitted changes"). The guard's error message should explicitly note that `.beads/issues.jsonl` is auto-handled and need not be stashed.
5. The JSONL diff must not be silently discarded. When `mindspec next` creates the bead worktree, the bead worktree reads through main's `.beads/` via the redirect file, so the new issues are visible inside the worktree without an explicit carry. The first `mindspec complete` on the bead branch will commit the JSONL as part of the bead's work via R2.

### R2: Run `bd export` at executor commit points

All executor-driven commits must include a fresh JSONL reflecting Dolt at commit time. In `internal/executor/mindspec_executor.go`:

1. `CommitAll` (or whichever helper wraps `git add -A && git commit`) must, before staging, run `bd export -o <main>/.beads/issues.jsonl`. Use `bd.FindBeadsDir()`-equivalent path resolution so the export always writes to the main repo's `.beads/`, regardless of which worktree the executor is running in.
2. If `bd export` fails, surface the error — do not silently fall through to staging stale state. Hook failures should be treated like any other pre-commit hook failure.
3. Applies to each executor path that ends in a git commit: `approve spec`, `approve plan`, `approve impl`, and `complete`.
4. The call must be idempotent and cheap — `bd export` against unchanged Dolt state is a byte-identical rewrite, so re-running it is safe.

This also closes the durability gap opened by R1: every mindspec-driven commit that lands on a PR-merged branch carries the point-in-time JSONL, making `git push` the off-machine durability guarantee even in projects without a Dolt remote.

### R3: Bootstrap mindspec-required beads config

Introduce `ensureBeadsConfig(root string) (*ConfigResult, error)` in `internal/bead/config.go` (or equivalent). It must:

1. Read `.beads/config.yaml` if present; create an empty one with a brief header comment if not.
2. Idempotently merge the mindspec-required keys:
   - `issue-prefix: "<project>"` — default to the repo directory name; do **not** overwrite an existing value
   - `types.custom: "gate"`
   - `status.custom: "resolved"`
   - `export.git-add: false` (belt-and-braces until bd v1.0.3+ ships the GH#3311 fix)
3. Preserve any user-authored keys and comments verbatim — structural-preserving YAML edit (e.g. `yaml.v3` Node-based mutation), not a full rewrite via `yaml.Marshal`.
4. Return a `ConfigResult` describing what was added, what was already correct, and any user-authored values left alone. Callers use this to print a summary.

Wire the helper into three call sites:

- `cmd/mindspec/init.go` → extend `bootstrap.Run` so a fresh `mindspec init` leaves `.beads/config.yaml` mindspec-ready (or leaves an informative no-op if `bd init` has not yet run).
- `cmd/mindspec/setup.go` (claude and copilot variants) → call after `chainBeadsSetup` so projects that ran `bd init` before `mindspec setup` still get the config patched.
- `cmd/mindspec/doctor.go` → in read-only mode, detect drift. With `--fix`, call `ensureBeadsConfig`.

**Intentional non-change:** `export.auto` is left at bd's default (`true`). Leaving auto-export on preserves durability — the JSONL stays fresh, the pre-commit hook keeps working, and R1 + R2 together make the resulting main-worktree dirt a non-blocker. Projects that want to disable auto-export for independent reasons can do so manually; mindspec does not mandate it either way.

### R4: Doctor checks for beads ↔ mindspec integration health

In `internal/doctor/beads.go`, add these checks. All are warnings (non-fatal) unless flagged otherwise.

1. **Config drift**: mindspec-required keys from R3 are missing or have unexpected values. Offer remediation in the message.
2. **Stray root-level `issues.jsonl`**: if `<root>/issues.jsonl` is tracked on any branch reachable from `HEAD`, warn. This is v1.0.2 GIT_DIR-pollution leakage. Suggest `git rm --cached issues.jsonl` on the affected branches. (Cleanup across branches is out of scope for this spec — see Out of Scope.)
3. **Durability risk**: if bd reports no configured Dolt remote AND `export.auto: false`, warn that ad-hoc `bd create` sessions outside mindspec's approve/complete flows will not refresh the JSONL — recommend either configuring a Dolt remote or reverting `export.auto` to `true`.
4. **bd version floor**: if `bd --version` reports < v1.0.2, warn. Earlier versions lack redirect fixes that mindspec's worktree model relies on.

## ADR Decisions

### ADR-0023

### 1. Eliminate both focus and lifecycle.yaml

No `.mindspec/focus` file anywhere — not on main, not in worktrees. No `lifecycle.yaml` in spec directories. All state queries go through beads.

CLI commands that currently write focus or lifecycle.yaml stop writing them. Commands that read them derive the same information from beads instead.

The spec directory contains only human-readable artifacts:
```
docs/specs/NNN-slug/
  spec.md        # specification document
  plan.md        # implementation plan
  context-pack.md  # generated context
  proofs/        # optional proof outputs
```

### 2. Epic metadata replaces lifecycle.yaml

Each spec's beads epic stores two metadata fields:

```bash
bd create --title="[SPEC 060-example] Feature Name" \
  --type=epic \
  --metadata='{"spec_num":60,"spec_title":"example"}'
```

- **`spec_num`** (int) — the numeric prefix, used for collision detection and ordering
- **`spec_title`** (kebab-case string) — the slug, used with `spec_num` to derive all paths

All identifiers and paths are derived from these two fields:

| Derived value | Formula |
|:-------------|:--------|
| `spec_id` | `fmt.Sprintf("%03d-%s", spec_num, spec_title)` |
| Spec branch | `spec/<spec_id>` |
| Spec worktree | `.worktrees/worktree-spec-<spec_id>` |
| Spec directory | `.mindspec/docs/specs/<spec_id>/` |
| Epic title | `[SPEC <spec_id>] <Human-Readable Title>` |

The `[SPEC NNN-slug]` title convention is retained for human readability but is not the primary lookup mechanism.

Discovery query:
```bash
bd list --type=epic --status=open --json
```
Then filter by `metadata.spec_num` for targeted lookups.

### 3. Phase derivation from bead statuses

The lifecycle phase for any spec is derived from its epic and bead statuses. Epic creation is the spec-approval gate — `spec approve` creates the epic, so its existence is the durable approval record.

| Condition | Derived phase |
|:----------|:-------------|
| No epic with matching `spec_num` | **spec** (draft, not yet approved) |
| Epic exists, no children | **plan** (spec approved, plan being drafted) |
| Epic exists, all children open (none claimed) | **plan** (plan approved, beads ready) |
| Epic exists, any child in_progress | **implement** |
| Epic exists, all children closed, epic open | **review** |
| Epic closed | **done** |
| No open epics for any spec | **idle** |

Edge cases:
- Some children closed, some open, none in_progress → **plan** (next bead ready to claim)
- Some children closed, some open, some blocked → **plan** (only blocked beads remain; agent must unblock or replan)
- Multiple children in_progress → **implement** (parallel beads, multiple agents)

### 4. Worktree-to-spec resolution by convention

Worktree paths are deterministic from IDs — no state file needed to record them:

| Context | Path convention |
|:--------|:---------------|
| Spec worktree | `.worktrees/worktree-spec-<specID>` |
| Bead worktree | `.worktrees/worktree-<beadID>` |
| Spec branch | `spec/<specID>` |
| Bead branch | `bead/<beadID>` |

When an agent starts on main, `mindspec instruct` queries beads for open epics, derives the phase, and determines which worktree(s) exist by convention. It then emits the CWD redirect.

When an agent is already in a worktree, the worktree name encodes the spec or bead ID. The CLI extracts it from the path or branch name.

### 5. Beads as the single state store

| Concern | Source of truth | Location |
|:--------|:---------------|:---------|
| Which specs are active | Beads epics (open) | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Spec-to-epic binding | Epic `metadata.spec_num` + `metadata.spec_title` | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Spec lifecycle phase | Derived from bead statuses | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Which bead is claimed | Bead status = in_progress | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Bead dependencies/blocking | Beads dependency graph | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Spec artifacts | spec.md, plan.md | Per-worktree filesystem (on branch) |

No state files on the filesystem. Beads (Dolt) is the single state authority. Spec artifacts (spec.md, plan.md) are versioned documents on branches, not state.

### 6. Updated invariants (supersedes ADR-0022 §4)

1. **Main is read-only** — no direct commits, no file writes. Only receives merges. *(unchanged)*
2. **One worktree per unit of work** — specs, beads, and bugfixes each get their own worktree. *(unchanged)*
3. **Worktrees are disposable** — created by `mindspec next`/`spec create`, removed by `mindspec complete`/`cleanup`. *(unchanged)*
4. **Context switch = worktree switch** — each worktree has its own filesystem state. *(unchanged)*
5. **No state files on filesystem** — no focus, no lifecycle.yaml. State is derived from beads + path conventions. *(changed)*
6. **Beads is the single state store** — lifecycle discovery, phase derivation, spec-epic binding, and work-readiness queries all go through beads. *(new)*

### ADR-0006

**Zero changes on main. All work happens on branches in worktrees. One PR per spec lifecycle.**

Every mindspec-managed change — spec, plan, and implementation — happens on a branch in a dedicated worktree. Nothing is committed directly to main. Changes reach main only through a merge (PR when a remote is configured, local merge otherwise).

### Branch topology

The spec/plan branch serves as the integration branch for the entire spec lifecycle:

```
main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────●──────
                                                      ↑ single PR
spec/NNN-slug ────●────────────────●──────●──────●────●
  (spec, plan)    ↑               ↑      ↑      ↑
                  │    bead/xxx ──●      │      │
                  │                      │      │
                  │    bead/yyy ─────────●      │
                  │                             │
                  │    bead/zzz ────────────────●
```

1. **`mindspec spec-init`** creates a `spec/NNN-slug` branch and worktree. All spec and plan work happens here.
2. **`/plan-approve`** approves the plan. The spec/plan worktree stays open — it becomes the integration point for implementation beads.
3. **`mindspec next`** creates `bead/<bead-id>` branches from `spec/NNN-slug` (not from main). Each impl bead gets its own worktree.
4. **`mindspec complete`** merges each impl bead back to `spec/NNN-slug` (not to main).
5. **When all beads are done**, a single PR merges `spec/NNN-slug` to main. This PR contains the complete body of work: spec, plan, and all implementation.

### Merge strategy (configurable)

| Strategy | Behavior | Use case |
|----------|----------|----------|
| `pr` | Push branch, create PR, wait for CI/review | Teams, production repos |
| `direct` | Local branch merge to main | Solo dev, local-only, no remote |
| `auto` | PR if remote exists, direct merge if local-only | Default |

### Enforcement

Enforcement is deterministic, not prompt-based. See ADR-0019 for the three-layer enforcement mechanism (git hooks, CLI guards, agent hooks).

title: [082-beads-artifact-handling] Bead 4: Wire `EnsureBeadsConfig` into init/setup + fix `mindspec spec create` redirect gap
description:
Applies ADR-0023 (bootstrap path) and implements the plan-time finding that `mindspec spec create` currently produces worktrees without `.beads/redirect`. The diagnosis and patch are scoped with a spike timebox; if diagnosis exceeds the timebox the wiring half ships alone and the redirect work splits into a follow-up spec.

**Context added from plan-time investigation**: during this spec's own Plan Mode transition, running `mindspec spec approve` failed with `bd list --type=epic failed: exit status 1` because the newly-created spec worktree had no `.beads/redirect` file — `bd` spawned its own empty Dolt server. Inspection of `internal/executor/mindspec_executor.go:InitSpecWorkspace` shows it correctly calls `bead.WorktreeCreate` (shells out to `bd worktree create`), but the redirect was not written. Either `bd worktree create` is not completing the redirect step when the branch already exists, or a later step in mindspec's flow overwrites the worktree's `.beads/`.

**Spike timebox for redirect diagnosis (Step 2 below)**: one agent session. If the root cause isn't conclusively identified in that window, file a follow-up bead for the patch and ship the wiring work alone — do not let diagnostic uncertainty block `EnsureBeadsConfig` from reaching users.

**Steps**
1. Reproduce the redirect-missing bug in an isolated integration test: run `mindspec spec create <id>` end-to-end in a temp repo with `bd` installed, then assert `<worktree>/.beads/redirect` exists and contains the correct relative path to the main repo's `.beads/`.
2. Diagnose (spike-timeboxed): enable `BD_DEBUG_ROUTING=1`, re-run, read the trace. Determine whether `bd worktree create` fails silently, is called with wrong args, or is called correctly but the redirect file is clobbered later. If timebox expires without conclusion, file follow-up bead and skip to Step 4.
3. Patch the root cause (only if Step 2 concluded). Likely shapes: (a) surface `bead.WorktreeCreate` errors that are currently swallowed, (b) post-worktree-create assertion in `InitSpecWorkspace` that the redirect file exists, with remediation if not, (c) fix an ordering bug where spec files are written to `.beads/` before `bd worktree create` runs.
4. Call `EnsureBeadsConfig(root, false)` from `cmd/mindspec/init.go:bootstrap.Run`. Print `ConfigResult` summary.
5. Call `EnsureBeadsConfig(root, false)` from `cmd/mindspec/setup.go`'s claude and copilot subcommands, after `chainBeadsSetup`/`chainBeadsSetupCodex`. Print summary.
6. Integration tests (temp-dir + fake `bd` on `PATH`): (a) `mindspec init` + real `bd init` leaves config mindspec-ready; (b) `mindspec setup claude` after a bare `bd init` patches the config; (c) second run is a no-op.

**Verification**
- [ ] `go test ./internal/bead/... -run Worktree` passes (including the new redirect-missing regression test, even if Step 3 is deferred — the test captures the bug either way).
- [ ] `go test ./internal/executor/... -run InitSpecWorkspace` passes.
- [ ] `go test ./cmd/mindspec/... -run "Init|Setup"` passes.
- [ ] Manual smoke: `mindspec spec create NNN-smoke` in a scratch repo — the new worktree has `.beads/redirect` and `bd list` from inside returns main's issues.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] Spec AC "fresh `mindspec init` + `bd init` produces `.beads/config.yaml` with the mindspec-required keys" — covered by integration test (a).
- [ ] Spec AC "running `mindspec init` a second time is a no-op on the config file" — covered by integration test (c).
- [ ] `mindspec spec create <id>` produces a worktree with a correct `.beads/redirect` file — regression test exists and passes (patch implemented here or deferred to follow-up bead per spike timebox).
- [ ] Open Question 2 from spec is resolved: redirect is the durability mechanism; this bead makes its creation reliable.

**Depends on**

| Which specs are active | Beads epics (open) | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Spec-to-epic binding | Epic `metadata.spec_num` + `metadata.spec_title` | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Spec lifecycle phase | Derived from bead statuses | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Which bead is claimed | Bead status = in_progress | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Bead dependencies/blocking | Beads dependency graph | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Spec artifacts | spec.md, plan.md | Per-worktree filesystem (on branch) |

No state files on the filesystem. Beads (Dolt) is the single state authority. Spec artifacts (spec.md, plan.md) are versioned documents on branches, not state.

### 6. Updated invariants (supersedes ADR-0022 §4)

1. **Main is read-only** — no direct commits, no file writes. Only receives merges. *(unchanged)*
2. **One worktree per unit of work** — specs, beads, and bugfixes each get their own worktree. *(unchanged)*
3. **Worktrees are disposable** — created by `mindspec next`/`spec create`, removed by `mindspec complete`/`cleanup`. *(unchanged)*
4. **Context switch = worktree switch** — each worktree has its own filesystem state. *(unchanged)*
5. **No state files on filesystem** — no focus, no lifecycle.yaml. State is derived from beads + path conventions. *(changed)*
6. **Beads is the single state store** — lifecycle discovery, phase derivation, spec-epic binding, and work-readiness queries all go through beads. *(new)*

### ADR-0006

**Zero changes on main. All work happens on branches in worktrees. One PR per spec lifecycle.**

Every mindspec-managed change — spec, plan, and implementation — happens on a branch in a dedicated worktree. Nothing is committed directly to main. Changes reach main only through a merge (PR when a remote is configured, local merge otherwise).

### Branch topology

The spec/plan branch serves as the integration branch for the entire spec lifecycle:

```
main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────●──────
                                                      ↑ single PR
spec/NNN-slug ────●────────────────●──────●──────●────●
  (spec, plan)    ↑               ↑      ↑      ↑
                  │    bead/xxx ──●      │      │
                  │                      │      │
                  │    bead/yyy ─────────●      │
                  │                             │
                  │    bead/zzz ────────────────●
```

1. **`mindspec spec-init`** creates a `spec/NNN-slug` branch and worktree. All spec and plan work happens here.
2. **`/plan-approve`** approves the plan. The spec/plan worktree stays open — it becomes the integration point for implementation beads.
3. **`mindspec next`** creates `bead/<bead-id>` branches from `spec/NNN-slug` (not from main). Each impl bead gets its own worktree.
4. **`mindspec complete`** merges each impl bead back to `spec/NNN-slug` (not to main).
5. **When all beads are done**, a single PR merges `spec/NNN-slug` to main. This PR contains the complete body of work: spec, plan, and all implementation.

### Merge strategy (configurable)

| Strategy | Behavior | Use case |
|----------|----------|----------|
| `pr` | Push branch, create PR, wait for CI/review | Teams, production repos |
| `direct` | Local branch merge to main | Solo dev, local-only, no remote |
| `auto` | PR if remote exists, direct merge if local-only | Default |

### Enforcement

Enforcement is deterministic, not prompt-based. See ADR-0019 for the three-layer enforcement mechanism (git hooks, CLI guards, agent hooks).

title: [082-beads-artifact-handling] Bead 2: Executor `bd export` at commit points
description:
Applies ADR-0006 (the JSONL must land in each PR-merged commit to preserve main-is-authoritative semantics) and ADR-0023/ADR-0025 (the committed JSONL must match Dolt at commit time — no drift).

**Steps**
1. Identify every executor code path that ends in a `git add -A && git commit`. Today: `internal/executor/mindspec_executor.go:CommitAll` wrapping `gitutil.CommitAll`. Used by `approve spec`, `approve plan`, `approve impl`, and `complete`.
2. Inline a "pre-stage step" in `CommitAll` (not a git hook — to avoid the naming overload): before the `git add -A` call, shell out `bd export -o <repoRoot>/.beads/issues.jsonl` with `cmd.Dir = repoRoot`. Per "Shared conventions", we let bd resolve the redirect rather than reimplementing `FindBeadsDir` in Go.
3. On `bd export` failure, return the error up through `CommitAll` — do not swallow. Caller's error handling already surfaces commit failures to the user.
4. Note in a code comment: bd's own pre-commit hook (`.beads/hooks/pre-commit`) also runs `bd export` via `exportJSONLForCommit`. The second export produces byte-identical output, so there is no duplication bug, but a future maintainer should not "optimize away" one of the two — the executor-level export guarantees the commit contains the current state even if the git hook is skipped (e.g., `--no-verify`).
5. Unit tests using `MockExecutor`: assert that each `approve`/`complete` call path invokes the new `bd export` step before `git add -A`.
6. Integration test: run a real `approve spec` against a temp-dir repo with a mutated Dolt state. Assert `git show --stat HEAD` includes `.beads/issues.jsonl` and `git cat-file blob HEAD:.beads/issues.jsonl` byte-matches a fresh `bd export` run.

**Verification**
- [ ] `go test ./internal/executor/...` passes new tests.
- [ ] `go test ./internal/approve/...` and `./internal/complete/...` pass (no regression).
- [ ] Manual smoke: `mindspec approve spec <throwaway>` in a scratch repo with a mutated Dolt state — the resulting commit contains the updated JSONL.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] Spec AC "after approve/complete, `git show --stat HEAD` includes `.beads/issues.jsonl` whenever Dolt changed" — covered by the integration test.
- [ ] Spec AC "committed JSONL is byte-identical to a fresh `bd export` at commit time" — covered by the integration test's `cat-file` comparison.
- [ ] No existing harness scenario regresses.

**Depends on**
None
design:
## Requirements

### R1: Treat `.beads/issues.jsonl` as a build artifact in the dirty-tree guard

The dirty-tree guard in `mindspec next` (currently in `internal/next/`) must distinguish build-artifact dirt from user-work dirt.

1. Snapshot the set of dirty paths (staged + unstaged) via `git status --porcelain`.
2. If `.beads/issues.jsonl` appears in the dirty set:
   - Run `bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl` to normalize the diff to current Dolt state (defensive against stale throttled exports).
   - Re-check `git status`. The JSONL may still be dirty (legitimate issue changes) or may have gone back to clean (throttled auto-export was writing stale content).
3. If the **only** remaining dirty path is `.beads/issues.jsonl`, proceed with the bead claim. The guard's job is to prevent losing user code/docs — it must not block on auto-generated files.
4. If other paths are also dirty, preserve current behavior (abort with "uncommitted changes"). The guard's error message should explicitly note that `.beads/issues.jsonl` is auto-handled and need not be stashed.
5. The JSONL diff must not be silently discarded. When `mindspec next` creates the bead worktree, the bead worktree reads through main's `.beads/` via the redirect file, so the new issues are visible inside the worktree without an explicit carry. The first `mindspec complete` on the bead branch will commit the JSONL as part of the bead's work via R2.

### R2: Run `bd export` at executor commit points

All executor-driven commits must include a fresh JSONL reflecting Dolt at commit time. In `internal/executor/mindspec_executor.go`:

1. `CommitAll` (or whichever helper wraps `git add -A && git commit`) must, before staging, run `bd export -o <main>/.beads/issues.jsonl`. Use `bd.FindBeadsDir()`-equivalent path resolution so the export always writes to the main repo's `.beads/`, regardless of which worktree the executor is running in.
2. If `bd export` fails, surface the error — do not silently fall through to staging stale state. Hook failures should be treated like any other pre-commit hook failure.
3. Applies to each executor path that ends in a git commit: `approve spec`, `approve plan`, `approve impl`, and `complete`.
4. The call must be idempotent and cheap — `bd export` against unchanged Dolt state is a byte-identical rewrite, so re-running it is safe.

This also closes the durability gap opened by R1: every mindspec-driven commit that lands on a PR-merged branch carries the point-in-time JSONL, making `git push` the off-machine durability guarantee even in projects without a Dolt remote.

### R3: Bootstrap mindspec-required beads config

Introduce `ensureBeadsConfig(root string) (*ConfigResult, error)` in `internal/bead/config.go` (or equivalent). It must:

1. Read `.beads/config.yaml` if present; create an empty one with a brief header comment if not.
2. Idempotently merge the mindspec-required keys:
   - `issue-prefix: "<project>"` — default to the repo directory name; do **not** overwrite an existing value
   - `types.custom: "gate"`
   - `status.custom: "resolved"`
   - `export.git-add: false` (belt-and-braces until bd v1.0.3+ ships the GH#3311 fix)
3. Preserve any user-authored keys and comments verbatim — structural-preserving YAML edit (e.g. `yaml.v3` Node-based mutation), not a full rewrite via `yaml.Marshal`.
4. Return a `ConfigResult` describing what was added, what was already correct, and any user-authored values left alone. Callers use this to print a summary.

Wire the helper into three call sites:

- `cmd/mindspec/init.go` → extend `bootstrap.Run` so a fresh `mindspec init` leaves `.beads/config.yaml` mindspec-ready (or leaves an informative no-op if `bd init` has not yet run).
- `cmd/mindspec/setup.go` (claude and copilot variants) → call after `chainBeadsSetup` so projects that ran `bd init` before `mindspec setup` still get the config patched.
- `cmd/mindspec/doctor.go` → in read-only mode, detect drift. With `--fix`, call `ensureBeadsConfig`.

**Intentional non-change:** `export.auto` is left at bd's default (`true`). Leaving auto-export on preserves durability — the JSONL stays fresh, the pre-commit hook keeps working, and R1 + R2 together make the resulting main-worktree dirt a non-blocker. Projects that want to disable auto-export for independent reasons can do so manually; mindspec does not mandate it either way.

### R4: Doctor checks for beads ↔ mindspec integration health

In `internal/doctor/beads.go`, add these checks. All are warnings (non-fatal) unless flagged otherwise.

1. **Config drift**: mindspec-required keys from R3 are missing or have unexpected values. Offer remediation in the message.
2. **Stray root-level `issues.jsonl`**: if `<root>/issues.jsonl` is tracked on any branch reachable from `HEAD`, warn. This is v1.0.2 GIT_DIR-pollution leakage. Suggest `git rm --cached issues.jsonl` on the affected branches. (Cleanup across branches is out of scope for this spec — see Out of Scope.)
3. **Durability risk**: if bd reports no configured Dolt remote AND `export.auto: false`, warn that ad-hoc `bd create` sessions outside mindspec's approve/complete flows will not refresh the JSONL — recommend either configuring a Dolt remote or reverting `export.auto` to `true`.
4. **bd version floor**: if `bd --version` reports < v1.0.2, warn. Earlier versions lack redirect fixes that mindspec's worktree model relies on.

## ADR Decisions

### ADR-0023

### 1. Eliminate both focus and lifecycle.yaml

No `.mindspec/focus` file anywhere — not on main, not in worktrees. No `lifecycle.yaml` in spec directories. All state queries go through beads.

CLI commands that currently write focus or lifecycle.yaml stop writing them. Commands that read them derive the same information from beads instead.

The spec directory contains only human-readable artifacts:
```
docs/specs/NNN-slug/
  spec.md        # specification document
  plan.md        # implementation plan
  context-pack.md  # generated context
  proofs/        # optional proof outputs
```

### 2. Epic metadata replaces lifecycle.yaml

Each spec's beads epic stores two metadata fields:

```bash
bd create --title="[SPEC 060-example] Feature Name" \
  --type=epic \
  --metadata='{"spec_num":60,"spec_title":"example"}'
```

- **`spec_num`** (int) — the numeric prefix, used for collision detection and ordering
- **`spec_title`** (kebab-case string) — the slug, used with `spec_num` to derive all paths

All identifiers and paths are derived from these two fields:

| Derived value | Formula |
|:-------------|:--------|
| `spec_id` | `fmt.Sprintf("%03d-%s", spec_num, spec_title)` |
| Spec branch | `spec/<spec_id>` |
| Spec worktree | `.worktrees/worktree-spec-<spec_id>` |
| Spec directory | `.mindspec/docs/specs/<spec_id>/` |
| Epic title | `[SPEC <spec_id>] <Human-Readable Title>` |

The `[SPEC NNN-slug]` title convention is retained for human readability but is not the primary lookup mechanism.

Discovery query:
```bash
bd list --type=epic --status=open --json
```
Then filter by `metadata.spec_num` for targeted lookups.

### 3. Phase derivation from bead statuses

The lifecycle phase for any spec is derived from its epic and bead statuses. Epic creation is the spec-approval gate — `spec approve` creates the epic, so its existence is the durable approval record.

| Condition | Derived phase |
|:----------|:-------------|
| No epic with matching `spec_num` | **spec** (draft, not yet approved) |
| Epic exists, no children | **plan** (spec approved, plan being drafted) |
| Epic exists, all children open (none claimed) | **plan** (plan approved, beads ready) |
| Epic exists, any child in_progress | **implement** |
| Epic exists, all children closed, epic open | **review** |
| Epic closed | **done** |
| No open epics for any spec | **idle** |

Edge cases:
- Some children closed, some open, none in_progress → **plan** (next bead ready to claim)
- Some children closed, some open, some blocked → **plan** (only blocked beads remain; agent must unblock or replan)
- Multiple children in_progress → **implement** (parallel beads, multiple agents)

### 4. Worktree-to-spec resolution by convention

Worktree paths are deterministic from IDs — no state file needed to record them:

| Context | Path convention |
|:--------|:---------------|
| Spec worktree | `.worktrees/worktree-spec-<specID>` |
| Bead worktree | `.worktrees/worktree-<beadID>` |
| Spec branch | `spec/<specID>` |
| Bead branch | `bead/<beadID>` |

When an agent starts on main, `mindspec instruct` queries beads for open epics, derives the phase, and determines which worktree(s) exist by convention. It then emits the CWD redirect.

When an agent is already in a worktree, the worktree name encodes the spec or bead ID. The CLI extracts it from the path or branch name.

### 5. Beads as the single state store

| Concern | Source of truth | Location |
|:--------|:---------------|:---------|
| Which specs are active | Beads epics (open) | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Spec-to-epic binding | Epic `metadata.spec_num` + `metadata.spec_title` | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Spec lifecycle phase | Derived from bead statuses | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Which bead is claimed | Bead status = in_progress | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Bead dependencies/blocking | Beads dependency graph | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Spec artifacts | spec.md, plan.md | Per-worktree filesystem (on branch) |

No state files on the filesystem. Beads (Dolt) is the single state authority. Spec artifacts (spec.md, plan.md) are versioned documents on branches, not state.

### 6. Updated invariants (supersedes ADR-0022 §4)

1. **Main is read-only** — no direct commits, no file writes. Only receives merges. *(unchanged)*
2. **One worktree per unit of work** — specs, beads, and bugfixes each get their own worktree. *(unchanged)*
3. **Worktrees are disposable** — created by `mindspec next`/`spec create`, removed by `mindspec complete`/`cleanup`. *(unchanged)*
4. **Context switch = worktree switch** — each worktree has its own filesystem state. *(unchanged)*
5. **No state files on filesystem** — no focus, no lifecycle.yaml. State is derived from beads + path conventions. *(changed)*
6. **Beads is the single state store** — lifecycle discovery, phase derivation, spec-epic binding, and work-readiness queries all go through beads. *(new)*

### ADR-0006

**Zero changes on main. All work happens on branches in worktrees. One PR per spec lifecycle.**

Every mindspec-managed change — spec, plan, and implementation — happens on a branch in a dedicated worktree. Nothing is committed directly to main. Changes reach main only through a merge (PR when a remote is configured, local merge otherwise).

### Branch topology

The spec/plan branch serves as the integration branch for the entire spec lifecycle:

```
main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────●──────
                                                      ↑ single PR
spec/NNN-slug ────●────────────────●──────●──────●────●
  (spec, plan)    ↑               ↑      ↑      ↑
                  │    bead/xxx ──●      │      │
                  │                      │      │
                  │    bead/yyy ─────────●      │
                  │                             │
                  │    bead/zzz ────────────────●
```

1. **`mindspec spec-init`** creates a `spec/NNN-slug` branch and worktree. All spec and plan work happens here.
2. **`/plan-approve`** approves the plan. The spec/plan worktree stays open — it becomes the integration point for implementation beads.
3. **`mindspec next`** creates `bead/<bead-id>` branches from `spec/NNN-slug` (not from main). Each impl bead gets its own worktree.
4. **`mindspec complete`** merges each impl bead back to `spec/NNN-slug` (not to main).
5. **When all beads are done**, a single PR merges `spec/NNN-slug` to main. This PR contains the complete body of work: spec, plan, and all implementation.

### Merge strategy (configurable)

| Strategy | Behavior | Use case |
|----------|----------|----------|
| `pr` | Push branch, create PR, wait for CI/review | Teams, production repos |
| `direct` | Local branch merge to main | Solo dev, local-only, no remote |
| `auto` | PR if remote exists, direct merge if local-only | Default |

### Enforcement

Enforcement is deterministic, not prompt-based. See ADR-0019 for the three-layer enforcement mechanism (git hooks, CLI guards, agent hooks).

title: [082-beads-artifact-handling] Bead 3: `EnsureBeadsConfig` helper with structural YAML merge
description:
Applies ADR-0023 (this is the bootstrap layer that ensures the projection/config stays in alignment with what mindspec's runtime reads at approve/complete time).

**Steps**
1. Create `internal/bead/config.go`. Export `EnsureBeadsConfig(root string, force bool) (*ConfigResult, error)`. `ConfigResult` fields: `Added []string`, `AlreadyCorrect []string`, `UserAuthored []ConfigDrift` (keys where user-authored value disagrees with mindspec's required value — left alone unless `force=true`), `CreatedFile bool`. Exported required-keys constant so Bead 5 can import it: `types.custom: "gate"`, `status.custom: "resolved"`, `export.git-add: false`, `issue-prefix: <default = filepath.Base(root)>`.
2. Implementation: use `yaml.v3` Node-based editing to preserve existing keys and comments byte-for-byte when the file exists; when it doesn't exist, create it via `yaml.Marshal` with a brief header comment (the fresh-file path has no structural-preservation concerns — document the exception inline). Write through temp file + `os.Rename` for atomicity.
3. Handling `issue-prefix`: never overwrite an existing value (user's project-naming choice). Only write when absent.
4. Handling `export.git-add: true` when set by the user: record in `UserAuthored`, leave untouched unless `force=true` (resolves Open Question 1 from the spec).
5. Unit tests for each core path: fresh file creation, merging into a partially-configured file, preserving non-mindspec keys, detecting user-authored drift, idempotence (second call byte-identical), `force=true` overrides.
6. **YAML edge-case golden tests** (per plan review): (a) top-of-file comment preserved, (b) comments interleaved between keys preserved, (c) block-style value preserved, (d) YAML anchor reference preserved.
7. Atomic-write test: simulate a write failure partway through `os.Rename` (or verify via inspection that `os.Rename` is atomic on the target filesystem) — ensures no partial-file corruption on interrupt.

**Verification**
- [ ] `go test ./internal/bead/... -run EnsureBeadsConfig` passes all cases including the four edge-case goldens.
- [ ] Round-trip test: a known input file through `EnsureBeadsConfig` with no changes required produces byte-identical output.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] `EnsureBeadsConfig` exists and compiles; exported symbols stable.
- [ ] Calling it twice produces byte-identical output (idempotence).
- [ ] User-authored keys outside the mindspec-required set are never touched.
- [ ] User-authored `export.git-add: true` is recorded in `UserAuthored`, not silently flipped (without `force`).
- [ ] All four YAML edge-case goldens pass.

**Depends on**
None


Each spec's beads epic stores two metadata fields:

```bash
bd create --title="[SPEC 060-example] Feature Name" \
  --type=epic \
  --metadata='{"spec_num":60,"spec_title":"example"}'
```

- **`spec_num`** (int) — the numeric prefix, used for collision detection and ordering
- **`spec_title`** (kebab-case string) — the slug, used with `spec_num` to derive all paths

All identifiers and paths are derived from these two fields:

| Derived value | Formula |
|:-------------|:--------|
| `spec_id` | `fmt.Sprintf("%03d-%s", spec_num, spec_title)` |
| Spec branch | `spec/<spec_id>` |
| Spec worktree | `.worktrees/worktree-spec-<spec_id>` |
| Spec directory | `.mindspec/docs/specs/<spec_id>/` |
| Epic title | `[SPEC <spec_id>] <Human-Readable Title>` |

The `[SPEC NNN-slug]` title convention is retained for human readability but is not the primary lookup mechanism.

Discovery query:
```bash
bd list --type=epic --status=open --json
```
Then filter by `metadata.spec_num` for targeted lookups.

### 3. Phase derivation from bead statuses

The lifecycle phase for any spec is derived from its epic and bead statuses. Epic creation is the spec-approval gate — `spec approve` creates the epic, so its existence is the durable approval record.

| Condition | Derived phase |
|:----------|:-------------|
| No epic with matching `spec_num` | **spec** (draft, not yet approved) |
| Epic exists, no children | **plan** (spec approved, plan being drafted) |
| Epic exists, all children open (none claimed) | **plan** (plan approved, beads ready) |
| Epic exists, any child in_progress | **implement** |
| Epic exists, all children closed, epic open | **review** |
| Epic closed | **done** |
| No open epics for any spec | **idle** |

Edge cases:
- Some children closed, some open, none in_progress → **plan** (next bead ready to claim)
- Some children closed, some open, some blocked → **plan** (only blocked beads remain; agent must unblock or replan)
- Multiple children in_progress → **implement** (parallel beads, multiple agents)

### 4. Worktree-to-spec resolution by convention

Worktree paths are deterministic from IDs — no state file needed to record them:

| Context | Path convention |
|:--------|:---------------|
| Spec worktree | `.worktrees/worktree-spec-<specID>` |
| Bead worktree | `.worktrees/worktree-<beadID>` |
| Spec branch | `spec/<specID>` |
| Bead branch | `bead/<beadID>` |

When an agent starts on main, `mindspec instruct` queries beads for open epics, derives the phase, and determines which worktree(s) exist by convention. It then emits the CWD redirect.

When an agent is already in a worktree, the worktree name encodes the spec or bead ID. The CLI extracts it from the path or branch name.

### 5. Beads as the single state store

| Concern | Source of truth | Location |
|:--------|:---------------|:---------|
| Which specs are active | Beads epics (open) | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Spec-to-epic binding | Epic `metadata.spec_num` + `metadata.spec_title` | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Spec lifecycle phase | Derived from bead statuses | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Which bead is claimed | Bead status = in_progress | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Bead dependencies/blocking | Beads dependency graph | `.beads/` (Dolt, shared) |
| Spec artifacts | spec.md, plan.md | Per-worktree filesystem (on branch) |

No state files on the filesystem. Beads (Dolt) is the single state authority. Spec artifacts (spec.md, plan.md) are versioned documents on branches, not state.

### 6. Updated invariants (supersedes ADR-0022 §4)

1. **Main is read-only** — no direct commits, no file writes. Only receives merges. *(unchanged)*
2. **One worktree per unit of work** — specs, beads, and bugfixes each get their own worktree. *(unchanged)*
3. **Worktrees are disposable** — created by `mindspec next`/`spec create`, removed by `mindspec complete`/`cleanup`. *(unchanged)*
4. **Context switch = worktree switch** — each worktree has its own filesystem state. *(unchanged)*
5. **No state files on filesystem** — no focus, no lifecycle.yaml. State is derived from beads + path conventions. *(changed)*
6. **Beads is the single state store** — lifecycle discovery, phase derivation, spec-epic binding, and work-readiness queries all go through beads. *(new)*

### ADR-0006

**Zero changes on main. All work happens on branches in worktrees. One PR per spec lifecycle.**

Every mindspec-managed change — spec, plan, and implementation — happens on a branch in a dedicated worktree. Nothing is committed directly to main. Changes reach main only through a merge (PR when a remote is configured, local merge otherwise).

### Branch topology

The spec/plan branch serves as the integration branch for the entire spec lifecycle:

```
main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────●──────
                                                      ↑ single PR
spec/NNN-slug ────●────────────────●──────●──────●────●
  (spec, plan)    ↑               ↑      ↑      ↑
                  │    bead/xxx ──●      │      │
                  │                      │      │
                  │    bead/yyy ─────────●      │
                  │                             │
                  │    bead/zzz ────────────────●
```

1. **`mindspec spec-init`** creates a `spec/NNN-slug` branch and worktree. All spec and plan work happens here.
2. **`/plan-approve`** approves the plan. The spec/plan worktree stays open — it becomes the integration point for implementation beads.
3. **`mindspec next`** creates `bead/<bead-id>` branches from `spec/NNN-slug` (not from main). Each impl bead gets its own worktree.
4. **`mindspec complete`** merges each impl bead back to `spec/NNN-slug` (not to main).
5. **When all beads are done**, a single PR merges `spec/NNN-slug` to main. This PR contains the complete body of work: spec, plan, and all implementation.

### Merge strategy (configurable)

| Strategy | Behavior | Use case |
|----------|----------|----------|
| `pr` | Push branch, create PR, wait for CI/review | Teams, production repos |
| `direct` | Local branch merge to main | Solo dev, local-only, no remote |
| `auto` | PR if remote exists, direct merge if local-only | Default |

### Enforcement

Enforcement is deterministic, not prompt-based. See ADR-0019 for the three-layer enforcement mechanism (git hooks, CLI guards, agent hooks).
